input.name: codex-code-review
gemini-code-review
input.prompt:
You are performing an ADVERSARIAL CODE REVIEW for Story 1-3: Deploy NAT Gateway and Public IP Resources.

## Your Role
You are a senior Terraform/Azure infrastructure reviewer. Find what's WRONG or MISSING. No lazy "looks good" reviews. Find 3-10 specific, actionable issues.

## Story Summary
As a DevOps engineer, deploy a NAT Gateway with static public IP and managed public IP resources so that private/public subnets have deterministic outbound IPs.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. NAT Gateway provisioned using AVM `Azure/avm-res-network-natgateway/azurerm` v0.2.1 with Standard SKU and 4-min idle timeout
2. Static Standard public IP and /30 IP prefix assigned to NAT Gateway
3. NAT Gateway associated with PrivateSubnet and PublicSubnet
4. Public IPs via for_each over local.public_ip_definitions with Standard SKU, Static, Regional, IPv4
5. Outputs exposed: NAT Gateway public IP address and prefix CIDR
6. Tags applied via local.common_tags

## Files Changed (git diff main...HEAD for terraform/ source files)

### terraform/natg.tf (NAT Gateway module + outputs)
```hcl
module "avm_nat_gateway" {
  source  = "Azure/avm-res-network-natgateway/azurerm"
  version = "0.2.1"
  name                    = local.nat_gateway_name
  location                = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.location
  resource_group_name     = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.name
  idle_timeout_in_minutes = 4
  sku_name                = "Standard"
  tags                    = local.common_tags
  enable_telemetry        = false
  zones                   = null
  public_ips = {
    "packer_image_natg_pip" = {
      name = local.nat_gateway_pip_name
    }
  }
  public_ip_configuration = {
    allocation_method       = "Static"
    sku                     = "Standard"
    zones                   = null
    ddos_protection_mode    = var.nat_gateway_pip_ddos_protection_mode
    idle_timeout_in_minutes = var.nat_gateway_pip_idle_timeout_in_minutes
  }
}

output "nat_gateway_id" {
  description = "The ID of the AVM NAT Gateway."
  value       = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id
}
output "nat_gateway_public_ip_address" {
  description = "The IP address of the Public IP created by the AVM NAT Gateway module."
  value       = module.avm_nat_gateway.public_ip_resource["packer_image_natg_pip"].ip_address
}
output "nat_gateway_public_ip_prefix_value" {
  description = "The CIDR of the Public IP Prefix associated with the NAT Gateway."
  value       = azurerm_public_ip_prefix.packer-image-natg-ipprefix.ip_prefix
}

resource "azurerm_nat_gateway_public_ip_prefix_association" "natg_prefix" {
  nat_gateway_id      = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id
  public_ip_prefix_id = azurerm_public_ip_prefix.packer-image-natg-ipprefix.id
}
```

### terraform/main.tf (Public IP Prefix)
```hcl
module "regions" {
  source  = "Azure/regions/azurerm"
  version = "~> 0.3"
}
resource "azurerm_public_ip_prefix" "packer-image-natg-ipprefix" {
  tags                = local.common_tags
  sku_tier            = "Regional"
  sku                 = "Standard"
  resource_group_name = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.name
  prefix_length       = 30
  name                = local.nat_gateway_pip_prefix_name
  location            = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.location
  ip_version          = "IPv4"
}
```

### terraform/networking.tf (VNet with subnet associations)
```hcl
module "virtualnetwork" {
  source  = "Azure/avm-res-network-virtualnetwork/azurerm"
  version = "~> 0.2"
  name                = local.vnet_name
  location            = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.location
  resource_group_name = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.name
  address_space       = var.vnet_address_space
  tags                = local.common_tags
  subnets = {
    GatewaySubnet = {
      name             = "GatewaySubnet"
      address_prefixes = var.vnet_subnet_gateway
    }
    AzureBastionSubnet = {
      name             = "AzureBastionSubnet"
      address_prefixes = var.vnet_azure_subnet_bastion
      network_security_group = { id = module.bastion_nsg_avm.resource_id }
    }
    PublicSubnet = {
      name             = local.subnet_public_name
      address_prefixes = var.vnet_subnet_public
      network_security_group = { id = module.public_nsg_avm.resource_id }
      nat_gateway = { id = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id }
      service_endpoints = ["Microsoft.ContainerRegistry","Microsoft.KeyVault","Microsoft.ServiceBus","Microsoft.Storage"]
    }
    PrivateSubnet = {
      name             = local.subnet_private_name
      address_prefixes = var.vnet_subnet_private
      network_security_group = { id = module.private_nsg_avm.resource_id }
      nat_gateway = { id = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id }
    }
  }
}
```

### terraform/public_ips.tf
```hcl
resource "azurerm_public_ip" "pips" {
  for_each = local.public_ip_definitions
  name                    = each.value.name
  resource_group_name     = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.name
  location                = var.location
  sku_tier                = "Regional"
  sku                     = "Standard"
  allocation_method       = "Static"
  ip_version              = "IPv4"
  idle_timeout_in_minutes = 4
  ddos_protection_mode    = "VirtualNetworkInherited"
  domain_name_label       = each.value.domain_name_label
  tags                    = each.value.apply_common_tags ? local.common_tags : null
}
```

### terraform/locals.tf
```hcl
locals {
  common_tags = { ... } # 24 tags
  public_ip_definitions = {
    "bastion" = {
      name              = local.pip_bastion_name
      domain_name_label = null
      apply_common_tags = true
    }
  }
}
```

### terraform/variables.tf (NAT Gateway PIP vars)
```hcl
variable "nat_gateway_pip_prefix_length" { type = number; default = 30 }
variable "nat_gateway_pip_sku" { type = string; default = "Standard" }
variable "nat_gateway_pip_allocation_method" { type = string; default = "Static" }
variable "nat_gateway_pip_zones" { type = list(string); default = null; nullable = true }
variable "nat_gateway_pip_ddos_protection_mode" { type = string; default = "VirtualNetworkInherited" }
variable "nat_gateway_pip_idle_timeout_in_minutes" { type = number; default = 4 }
```

### terraform/terraform.tfvars
```hcl
nat_gateway_pip_prefix_length           = 30
nat_gateway_pip_sku                     = "Standard"
nat_gateway_pip_allocation_method       = "Static"
nat_gateway_pip_zones                   = null
nat_gateway_pip_ddos_protection_mode    = "VirtualNetworkInherited"
nat_gateway_pip_idle_timeout_in_minutes = 4
```

## Review Focus Areas
1. **AC Validation**: Is each AC actually implemented?
2. **Unused Variables**: `nat_gateway_pip_sku`, `nat_gateway_pip_allocation_method`, `nat_gateway_pip_zones`, `nat_gateway_pip_prefix_length` — are these actually referenced in any resource?
3. **Location Inconsistency**: `public_ips.tf` uses `var.location` while other resources use `data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.location` — is this a bug?
4. **Destroy/Recreate Risk**: IP prefix name changed from hardcoded to local
5. **Code Quality**: Terraform best practices, Azure best practices
6. **Security**: DDoS protection, public IP exposure
7. **Resource Organization**: Outputs split across natg.tf and outputs.tf

## Output Format
```
## 🔴 CRITICAL ISSUES (must fix)
- [issue with file:line reference]

## 🟡 MEDIUM ISSUES (should fix)
- [issue]

## 🟢 LOW ISSUES (nice to fix)
- [issue]

## ✅ VERIFIED ACs
- AC #1: [status]
...
```

Be thorough. Be adversarial. Find real problems.

input.name: copilot-code-review
input.prompt:
You are performing an ADVERSARIAL CODE REVIEW for Story 1-3: Deploy NAT Gateway and Public IP Resources. You are simulating the perspective of GitHub Copilot — focus on code patterns, unused code, consistency, and pragmatic engineering issues.

## Your Role
Senior Terraform/Azure infrastructure reviewer. Find what's WRONG or MISSING. No lazy "looks good" reviews. Find 3-10 specific, actionable issues.

## Story: Deploy NAT Gateway with static public IP and managed public IP resources.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. NAT Gateway provisioned using AVM v0.2.1 with Standard SKU and 4-min idle timeout
2. Static Standard public IP and /30 IP prefix assigned to NAT Gateway
3. NAT Gateway associated with PrivateSubnet and PublicSubnet
4. Public IPs via for_each over local.public_ip_definitions
5. Outputs exposed: NAT Gateway public IP address and prefix CIDR
6. Tags applied via local.common_tags

## Key Files to Review

### terraform/natg.tf
```hcl
module "avm_nat_gateway" {
  source  = "Azure/avm-res-network-natgateway/azurerm"
  version = "0.2.1"
  name                    = local.nat_gateway_name
  location                = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.location
  resource_group_name     = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.name
  idle_timeout_in_minutes = 4
  sku_name                = "Standard"
  tags                    = local.common_tags
  enable_telemetry        = false
  zones                   = null
  public_ips = {
    "packer_image_natg_pip" = { name = local.nat_gateway_pip_name }
  }
  public_ip_configuration = {
    allocation_method       = "Static"
    sku                     = "Standard"
    zones                   = null
    ddos_protection_mode    = var.nat_gateway_pip_ddos_protection_mode
    idle_timeout_in_minutes = var.nat_gateway_pip_idle_timeout_in_minutes
  }
}
output "nat_gateway_id" {
  description = "The ID of the AVM NAT Gateway."
  value       = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id
}
output "nat_gateway_public_ip_address" {
  description = "The IP address of the Public IP created by the AVM NAT Gateway module."
  value       = module.avm_nat_gateway.public_ip_resource["packer_image_natg_pip"].ip_address
}
output "nat_gateway_public_ip_prefix_value" {
  description = "The CIDR of the Public IP Prefix associated with the NAT Gateway."
  value       = azurerm_public_ip_prefix.packer-image-natg-ipprefix.ip_prefix
}
resource "azurerm_nat_gateway_public_ip_prefix_association" "natg_prefix" {
  nat_gateway_id      = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id
  public_ip_prefix_id = azurerm_public_ip_prefix.packer-image-natg-ipprefix.id
}
```

### terraform/main.tf
```hcl
module "regions" {
  source  = "Azure/regions/azurerm"
  version = "~> 0.3"
}
resource "azurerm_public_ip_prefix" "packer-image-natg-ipprefix" {
  tags                = local.common_tags
  sku_tier            = "Regional"
  sku                 = "Standard"
  resource_group_name = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.name
  prefix_length       = 30
  name                = local.nat_gateway_pip_prefix_name
  location            = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.location
  ip_version          = "IPv4"
}
```

### terraform/networking.tf
```hcl
module "virtualnetwork" {
  source  = "Azure/avm-res-network-virtualnetwork/azurerm"
  version = "~> 0.2"
  name                = local.vnet_name
  location            = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.location
  resource_group_name = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.name
  address_space       = var.vnet_address_space
  tags                = local.common_tags
  subnets = {
    GatewaySubnet = {
      name             = "GatewaySubnet"
      address_prefixes = var.vnet_subnet_gateway
    }
    AzureBastionSubnet = {
      name             = "AzureBastionSubnet"
      address_prefixes = var.vnet_azure_subnet_bastion
      network_security_group = { id = module.bastion_nsg_avm.resource_id }
    }
    PublicSubnet = {
      name             = local.subnet_public_name
      address_prefixes = var.vnet_subnet_public
      network_security_group = { id = module.public_nsg_avm.resource_id }
      nat_gateway = { id = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id }
      service_endpoints = ["Microsoft.ContainerRegistry","Microsoft.KeyVault","Microsoft.ServiceBus","Microsoft.Storage"]
    }
    PrivateSubnet = {
      name             = local.subnet_private_name
      address_prefixes = var.vnet_subnet_private
      network_security_group = { id = module.private_nsg_avm.resource_id }
      nat_gateway = { id = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id }
    }
  }
}
```

### terraform/public_ips.tf
```hcl
resource "azurerm_public_ip" "pips" {
  for_each = local.public_ip_definitions
  name                    = each.value.name
  resource_group_name     = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.name
  location                = var.location
  sku_tier                = "Regional"
  sku                     = "Standard"
  allocation_method       = "Static"
  ip_version              = "IPv4"
  idle_timeout_in_minutes = 4
  ddos_protection_mode    = "VirtualNetworkInherited"
  domain_name_label       = each.value.domain_name_label
  tags                    = each.value.apply_common_tags ? local.common_tags : null
}
```

### terraform/variables.tf (NAT Gateway PIP section)
```hcl
variable "nat_gateway_pip_prefix_length" { type = number; default = 30 }
variable "nat_gateway_pip_sku" { type = string; default = "Standard" }
variable "nat_gateway_pip_allocation_method" { type = string; default = "Static" }
variable "nat_gateway_pip_zones" { type = list(string); default = null; nullable = true }
variable "nat_gateway_pip_ddos_protection_mode" { type = string; default = "VirtualNetworkInherited" }
variable "nat_gateway_pip_idle_timeout_in_minutes" { type = number; default = 4 }
```

## Specific Review Questions
1. Variables `nat_gateway_pip_sku`, `nat_gateway_pip_allocation_method`, `nat_gateway_pip_zones`, `nat_gateway_pip_prefix_length` — are these actually referenced anywhere in the terraform code? Or are they dead variables?
2. `public_ips.tf` uses `var.location` while `main.tf`, `natg.tf`, `networking.tf` use `data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.location` — inconsistency?
3. `main.tf` has `prefix_length = 30` hardcoded instead of using `var.nat_gateway_pip_prefix_length` — why declare the variable if not used?
4. The resource label `packer-image-natg-ipprefix` contains hyphens — valid but unconventional for Terraform resource labels
5. Outputs in natg.tf vs outputs.tf — scattered organization
6. `public_ip_configuration.idle_timeout_in_minutes` in natg.tf is set to `var.nat_gateway_pip_idle_timeout_in_minutes` (default 4) but tfvars also sets it to 4 — but story Dev Notes say the PIP idle timeout is 30 min. Which is correct?

Wait — check the Dev Notes carefully: "idle timeout from var.nat_gateway_pip_idle_timeout_in_minutes (30 min)" but tfvars says 4. Is the tfvars value wrong?

## Output Format
```
## 🔴 CRITICAL ISSUES (must fix)
- [issue with file reference]

## 🟡 MEDIUM ISSUES (should fix)  
- [issue]

## 🟢 LOW ISSUES (nice to fix)
- [issue]

## ✅ VERIFIED ACs
- AC #1: [status]
...
```

Be thorough and adversarial.

input.name: grok-code-review
input.prompt:
You are performing an ADVERSARIAL CODE REVIEW for Story 1-3: Deploy NAT Gateway and Public IP Resources. You bring a contrarian, fact-based perspective. Challenge assumptions. Find what others might miss.

## Story: Deploy NAT Gateway with static public IP and managed public IP resources.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. NAT Gateway provisioned using AVM v0.2.1 with Standard SKU and 4-min idle timeout
2. Static Standard public IP and /30 IP prefix assigned to NAT Gateway
3. NAT Gateway associated with PrivateSubnet and PublicSubnet
4. Public IPs via for_each over local.public_ip_definitions
5. Outputs exposed: NAT Gateway public IP address and prefix CIDR
6. Tags applied via local.common_tags

## Architecture Context
- Azure region: eastus2, VNet: 10.248.0.0/20
- GatewaySubnet: 10.248.0.0/22, PublicSubnet: 10.248.4.0/22, BastionSubnet: 10.248.8.0/26, PrivateSubnet: 10.248.9.0/24
- NAT Gateway provides deterministic outbound IPs for allowlisting
- No zones configured (null) for NAT Gateway and PIPs

## Key Code (all new files in this branch)

### terraform/natg.tf
```hcl
module "avm_nat_gateway" {
  source  = "Azure/avm-res-network-natgateway/azurerm"
  version = "0.2.1"
  name                    = local.nat_gateway_name
  location                = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.location
  resource_group_name     = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.name
  idle_timeout_in_minutes = 4
  sku_name                = "Standard"
  tags                    = local.common_tags
  enable_telemetry        = false
  zones                   = null
  public_ips = {
    "packer_image_natg_pip" = { name = local.nat_gateway_pip_name }
  }
  public_ip_configuration = {
    allocation_method       = "Static"
    sku                     = "Standard"
    zones                   = null
    ddos_protection_mode    = var.nat_gateway_pip_ddos_protection_mode
    idle_timeout_in_minutes = var.nat_gateway_pip_idle_timeout_in_minutes
  }
}
output "nat_gateway_id" { value = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id }
output "nat_gateway_public_ip_address" { value = module.avm_nat_gateway.public_ip_resource["packer_image_natg_pip"].ip_address }
output "nat_gateway_public_ip_prefix_value" { value = azurerm_public_ip_prefix.packer-image-natg-ipprefix.ip_prefix }
resource "azurerm_nat_gateway_public_ip_prefix_association" "natg_prefix" {
  nat_gateway_id      = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id
  public_ip_prefix_id = azurerm_public_ip_prefix.packer-image-natg-ipprefix.id
}
```

### terraform/main.tf
```hcl
module "regions" { source = "Azure/regions/azurerm"; version = "~> 0.3" }
resource "azurerm_public_ip_prefix" "packer-image-natg-ipprefix" {
  tags = local.common_tags; sku_tier = "Regional"; sku = "Standard"
  resource_group_name = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.name
  prefix_length = 30; name = local.nat_gateway_pip_prefix_name
  location = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.location; ip_version = "IPv4"
}
```

### terraform/networking.tf (subnet associations)
```hcl
PublicSubnet = {
  name = local.subnet_public_name; address_prefixes = var.vnet_subnet_public
  network_security_group = { id = module.public_nsg_avm.resource_id }
  nat_gateway = { id = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id }
  service_endpoints = ["Microsoft.ContainerRegistry","Microsoft.KeyVault","Microsoft.ServiceBus","Microsoft.Storage"]
}
PrivateSubnet = {
  name = local.subnet_private_name; address_prefixes = var.vnet_subnet_private
  network_security_group = { id = module.private_nsg_avm.resource_id }
  nat_gateway = { id = module.avm_nat_gateway.resource_id }
}
```

### terraform/public_ips.tf
```hcl
resource "azurerm_public_ip" "pips" {
  for_each = local.public_ip_definitions
  name = each.value.name; resource_group_name = data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.name
  location = var.location  # <-- NOTE: uses var.location, not data source
  sku_tier = "Regional"; sku = "Standard"; allocation_method = "Static"
  ip_version = "IPv4"; idle_timeout_in_minutes = 4
  ddos_protection_mode = "VirtualNetworkInherited"
  domain_name_label = each.value.domain_name_label
  tags = each.value.apply_common_tags ? local.common_tags : null
}
```

### terraform/variables.tf — These 4 variables are declared but NOT referenced in any resource:
- `nat_gateway_pip_prefix_length` (hardcoded as 30 in main.tf instead)
- `nat_gateway_pip_sku` (hardcoded as "Standard" in natg.tf public_ip_configuration)
- `nat_gateway_pip_allocation_method` (hardcoded as "Static" in natg.tf)
- `nat_gateway_pip_zones` (hardcoded as null in natg.tf)

### Dev Notes Contradiction
Dev Notes say "idle timeout from var.nat_gateway_pip_idle_timeout_in_minutes (30 min)" but terraform.tfvars sets this to 4. The variable default is also 4. So the dev notes claim 30 min but the actual value is 4. Is this an error in dev notes, or should the tfvars be 30?

## Review Focus
1. Dead/unused variables — are 4 of 6 NAT Gateway PIP variables actually dead code?
2. Location inconsistency — var.location vs data.azurerm_resource_group.packer_rg.location
3. Availability zones = null — is this appropriate for production? What's the SLA impact?
4. /30 prefix gives only 4 IPs — is this enough for the workload?
5. NAT Gateway on PublicSubnet — is this architecturally sound? PublicSubnet typically has direct internet access
6. The IP prefix resource label has hyphens — Terraform convention prefers underscores
7. Outputs scattered across files — maintainability concern

## Output Format
```
## 🔴 CRITICAL ISSUES (must fix)
- [issue]

## 🟡 MEDIUM ISSUES (should fix)
- [issue]

## 🟢 LOW ISSUES (nice to fix)
- [issue]

## ✅ VERIFIED ACs
- AC #1: [status]
...

## 🤔 ARCHITECTURAL QUESTIONS
- [question that challenges assumptions]
```

Be contrarian. Challenge everything. Find what linear reviewers miss.
